body:
## Summary
- Render Giscus as a fixed discussion drawer instead of an after-body block
- Add a bottom-right trigger with pill/bot modes and Sepo wave assets
- Support lazy Giscus mounting, theme updates, close controls, and resizable drawer width
- Document local testing with self-evolving/repo-discussions

## Testing
- npm run check

comments.body:
## Rubrics Review

| Total Score | Verdict | Rubrics Scored |
| -- | -- | -- |
| 100 | PASS | 9 |

| Dimension | Rubric | Result | Score | Evidence |
| -- | -- | -- | -- | -- |
| coding_workflow / generic | Reuse existing code | pass | 5/5 | Reuses the existing `Comments` component, `enumEnv`, Giscus data attributes, and SPA `window.addCleanup` lifecycle instead of adding parallel plumbing. |
| coding_workflow / generic | Make surgical changes | pass | 5/5 | Live PR diff is scoped to Giscus comments UI/config/docs plus two Sepo trigger assets. |
| coding_workflow / generic | Keep docs in sync | pass | 5/5 | README documents drawer behavior, `GISCUS_TRIGGER_MODE`, and local Giscus testing config. |
| coding_workflow / generic | Prefer explicit structured inputs | pass | 5/5 | Adds typed `triggerMode?: "pill" \| "bot"` and validates `GISCUS_TRIGGER_MODE` with `enumEnv`. |
| coding_workflow / generic | Minimal defaults, native escape hatches | pass | 5/5 | Keeps customization to one common-case trigger mode option with default `pill`; no broad settings framework added. |
| coding_workflow / generic | Name config by capability | pass | 5/5 | `GISCUS_TRIGGER_MODE` names the exposed Giscus trigger capability clearly. |
| communication / generic | Make setup guidance actionable | pass | 5/5 | README includes a copyable local testing command using `self-evolving/repo-discussions`. |
| communication / generic | Write audience-first documentation | pass | 5/5 | The docs lead with enabling comments and what users see, then give testing details. |
| coding_workflow / generic | No per-file inventory tables in docs | pass | 5/5 | README update avoids file inventory tables and documents behavior/config instead. |

## Notes

- Route/authorization, privileged workflow, preview, retry, rubric-management, and review-synthesis rubrics were not scored because this PR changes a Quartz/Giscus site UI, not agent workflow control flow.
- Verification: `npm run check` passed on PR head `f8c7ead0e0146976543c11f71f12619b2ffe4aaa` after installing dependencies with Node 22/npm 10.

## Findings

- **INFO:** No rubric-specific follow-up needed.

## Final Rubric Verdict

PASS

---
_Run: `codex` / `gpt-5.5/xhigh` / `high`_

## AI Review Synthesis

<!-- sepo-agent-review-synthesis -->
<!-- sepo-agent-review-synthesis-head: f8c7ead0e0146976543c11f71f12619b2ffe4aaa -->

> Dual-agent review by **Claude** and **Codex**.
> Requested by @lolipopshock.

## Summary of PR/Issue
- PR #17 adds a Giscus discussion drawer for literature pages.
- It replaces the previous in-page comments block with a fixed trigger that opens a right-side drawer.
- The PR also adds `pill` and `bot` trigger modes, lazy Giscus mounting, resizable drawer width, Sepo wave assets, and README guidance for local Giscus testing.
- The implementation is scoped to the comments UI/config/docs path and reuses existing Quartz/Sepo component patterns.

## Review
The PR is close, but two concrete accessibility issues should be fixed before shipping. Reviewers agreed most strongly on the resize-handle focus regression; one reviewer also raised a current label-in-name issue on the default trigger.

| Issue | Severity | Description |
| -- | -- | -- |
| Trigger accessible name mismatches visible text | WARNING | Default pill mode visibly says `Chat with Sepo`, but the button’s accessible name is `Discuss this note`, which can break voice-control activation. |
| Resize handle has no visible keyboard focus | WARNING | The drawer resize handle is keyboard-focusable and keyboard-operable, but its `:focus-visible` rule removes the outline with no replacement. |
| Mobile drawer modality needs maintainer choice | INFO | One reviewer noted the mobile drawer visually behaves like a modal while using complementary-region semantics and no focus containment; this is a design/accessibility follow-up rather than required automation work. |

## Progress
- Rubrics review passed with no rubric-specific follow-up.
- Reviewers reported `npm run check`; one reviewer also reported `npm run build` and a Giscus-enabled Quartz build passed.
- Existing inline comments and review threads were empty before synthesis.
- Posted inline comments for [`Comments.tsx:51`](https://github.com/self-evolving/literature-template/pull/17#discussion_r3360828343) and [`comments.scss:173`](https://github.com/self-evolving/literature-template/pull/17#discussion_r3360828703).
- The “trigger on every page” concern is not carried forward: current config gates `sepo-comments` with `condition: library-page`.

## Issue Details

<details>
<summary>Trigger accessible name mismatches visible text</summary>

**Cause:** [`quartz/components/Comments.tsx#L51`](https://github.com/self-evolving/literature-template/blob/f8c7ead0e0146976543c11f71f12619b2ffe4aaa/quartz/components/Comments.tsx#L51) sets `aria-label="Discuss this note"`, while the visible pill label at line 81 is `Chat with Sepo`.

**Candidate solutions:** Make the accessible name start with `Chat with Sepo`, or omit the overriding `aria-label` in pill mode and let the visible label name the button.

**Comments:** Inline feedback was posted because this is current, concrete, and line-specific.

</details>

<details>
<summary>Resize handle has no visible keyboard focus</summary>

**Cause:** [`quartz/components/styles/comments.scss#L172-L173`](https://github.com/self-evolving/literature-template/blob/f8c7ead0e0146976543c11f71f12619b2ffe4aaa/quartz/components/styles/comments.scss#L172) removes the focus outline. The handle is focusable in `Comments.tsx` and supports Arrow/Home/End resizing in `comments.inline.ts`.

**Candidate solutions:** Keep the default outline, or add a visible custom `:focus-visible` state such as a contrasting vertical highlight, background, or box shadow.

**Comments:** Both code reviewers flagged this issue; inline feedback was posted.

</details>

<details>
<summary>Mobile drawer modality needs maintainer choice</summary>

**Cause:** On mobile, the drawer uses a full-width panel, backdrop, and body scroll lock, while the panel remains `role="complementary"` and does not trap focus.

**Candidate solutions:** Either make the mobile drawer a real dialog with modal semantics and focus containment, or remove the modal-like backdrop/scroll-lock behavior and keep it non-modal.

**Comments:** This is valid follow-up context, but it needs a maintainer choice about intended mobile behavior.

</details>

## Recommended Next Step
FIX_PR: The required unresolved issues are small, local accessibility fixes that are safe for an automated fix-pr pass.

## Final Verdict
MINOR_ISSUES

## Action Items
- [ ] Update the trigger accessible name so default pill mode includes the visible `Chat with Sepo` label.
- [ ] Add a visible keyboard focus indicator for the resize handle.

---
_Run: `codex` / `gpt-5.5/xhigh` / `xhigh`_

## Rubrics Update

No changes were committed to [`agent/rubrics`](https://github.com/self-evolving/literature-template/tree/agent/rubrics) from PR #17.

no rubric changes

I found no trusted PR conversation content that warranted a new or updated durable rubric. The substantive feedback was agent-authored accessibility review output, and the only human comments from `lolipopshock` were `/review` commands. Existing rubrics already cover the durable workflow/docs preferences shown by the PR, and bot feedback was not explicitly endorsed as a team preference.

---
_Run: `codex` / `gpt-5.5/xhigh` / `medium`_

The local `npm run check` did not reach PR-specific type or formatting checks because this checkout does not have dependencies installed; the failures are missing modules/types across the whole repo. I’ll include that as verification context, not as a rubric failure, because the PR body and prior run artifacts are the relevant test evidence here.## Rubrics Review

| Total Score | Verdict | Rubrics Scored |
| -- | -- | -- |
| 100 | PASS | 9 |

| Dimension | Rubric | Result | Score | Evidence |
| -- | -- | -- | -- | -- |
| coding_workflow / generic | Reuse existing code | pass | 5/5 | Keeps the existing `Comments` component/Giscus data-attribute path and reuses `enumEnv` plus `window.addCleanup` lifecycle handling. |
| coding_workflow / generic | Make surgical changes | pass | 5/5 | Diff is scoped to Giscus drawer UI/config/docs and two Sepo trigger assets. |
| coding_workflow / generic | Keep docs in sync | pass | 5/5 | README documents drawer behavior, `GISCUS_TRIGGER_MODE`, and local Giscus testing configuration. |
| coding_workflow / generic | Prefer explicit structured inputs | pass | 5/5 | Adds typed `triggerMode?: "pill" \| "bot"` and validates `GISCUS_TRIGGER_MODE` via `enumEnv`. |
| coding_workflow / generic | Minimal defaults, native escape hatches | pass | 5/5 | Adds one narrow trigger-mode option with default `pill`, without introducing a broad settings framework. |
| coding_workflow / generic | Name config by capability | pass | 5/5 | `GISCUS_TRIGGER_MODE` names the exposed Giscus trigger behavior directly. |
| communication / generic | Make setup guidance actionable | pass | 5/5 | README includes a copyable local testing command using `self-evolving/repo-discussions`. |
| communication / generic | Write audience-first documentation | pass | 5/5 | Docs lead with enabling comments and visible behavior, then provide testing details. |
| coding_workflow / generic | No per-file inventory tables in docs | pass | 5/5 | README update documents behavior/configuration rather than adding a per-file inventory. |

## Notes

- Current PR head is `08782e8115c095ca26901674bde1178cf04f6720`; the PR is merged via `76d3c410ce0e20d82705838299db795fa08090c9`.
- Earlier accessibility warnings are not carried forward: the current diff removes the trigger’s mismatched overriding `aria-label`, and `.comments-resize-handle:focus-visible` now has a visible background/box-shadow focus state.
- Local `npm run check` could not verify this checkout because dependencies are not installed; failures were missing modules/types across the repo, not PR-specific diagnostics.

## Findings

- **INFO:** No rubric-specific follow-up needed.

## Final Rubric Verdict

PASS

---
_Run: `codex` / `gpt-5.5/xhigh` / `high`_

## AI Review Synthesis

<!-- sepo-agent-review-synthesis -->
<!-- sepo-agent-review-synthesis-head: 08782e8115c095ca26901674bde1178cf04f6720 -->

> Dual-agent review by **Claude** and **Codex**.
> Requested by @lolipopshock.

## Summary of PR/Issue
- PR #17 adds a fixed Giscus discussion drawer for literature pages, replacing the old after-body comment block.
- It adds a bottom-right trigger with `pill` and `bot` modes, lazy Giscus mounting, drawer close controls, theme updates, and desktop resizing.
- It also documents `GISCUS_TRIGGER_MODE` and local testing with `self-evolving/repo-discussions`.
- The PR is already merged at `08782e8115c095ca26901674bde1178cf04f6720`.

## Review
The first-pass accessibility warnings were fixed, but the current merged head still has two concrete accessibility follow-ups. The remaining mobile modality concern is real but needs maintainer intent before automation should choose a direction.

| Issue | Severity | Description |
| -- | -- | -- |
| Focusable resize separator lacks ARIA range state | WARNING | The keyboard-resizable `role="separator"` has no `aria-valuenow`, so assistive tech cannot report the current drawer width. |
| Comments drawer ignores reduced-motion preference | WARNING | The shimmer, drawer/trigger transitions, and animated Sepo avatar run without a `prefers-reduced-motion` path. |
| Mobile drawer modality needs maintainer choice | INFO | Mobile uses backdrop and body scroll lock while retaining non-modal `complementary` semantics and no focus trap. |

## Progress
- Rubrics review passed with no rubric-specific follow-up.
- Prior warnings are fixed: the trigger no longer overrides `Chat with Sepo`, and the resize handle now has a visible focus state.
- Reviewer verification reported `npm run check`; Codex also reported `npm ci` and a Giscus-enabled build passed.
- Posted inline comments for `Comments.tsx:96` and `comments.scss:291`.
- No threads were resolved or minimized; older same-agent threads are stale, but `viewerCanResolve` is false.

## Issue Details

<details>
<summary>Focusable resize separator lacks ARIA range state</summary>

**Cause:** [`Comments.tsx#L91-L97`](https://github.com/self-evolving/literature-template/blob/08782e8115c095ca26901674bde1178cf04f6720/quartz/components/Comments.tsx#L91-L97) makes the resize handle a focusable `role="separator"`, and [`comments.inline.ts#L148-L151`](https://github.com/self-evolving/literature-template/blob/08782e8115c095ca26901674bde1178cf04f6720/quartz/components/scripts/comments.inline.ts#L148-L151) changes width without updating ARIA range state. W3C APG notes `aria-valuenow` is required for focusable separators.

**Candidate solutions:** Add `aria-valuemin`, `aria-valuemax`, and initial `aria-valuenow`; update `aria-valuenow` whenever `setPanelWidth` changes the drawer width.

**Comments:** Both code reviewers flagged this. Inline feedback was posted.

</details>

<details>
<summary>Comments drawer ignores reduced-motion preference</summary>

**Cause:** [`comments.scss#L291`](https://github.com/self-evolving/literature-template/blob/08782e8115c095ca26901674bde1178cf04f6720/quartz/components/styles/comments.scss#L291) runs an infinite loading shimmer, and [`comments.inline.ts#L212-L229`](https://github.com/self-evolving/literature-template/blob/08782e8115c095ca26901674bde1178cf04f6720/quartz/components/scripts/comments.inline.ts#L212-L229) swaps to the animated WebP without checking reduced-motion preference.

**Candidate solutions:** Add a `prefers-reduced-motion: reduce` media query to suppress the shimmer and nonessential transform transitions; guard `playSepoWave` with `matchMedia`.

**Comments:** Claude raised this as a warning; Codex did not. Current code supports carrying it as a warning.

</details>

<details>
<summary>Mobile drawer modality needs maintainer choice</summary>

**Cause:** Mobile uses a full-width panel, backdrop, and body scroll lock, but keeps `role="complementary"` and does not contain focus.

**Candidate solutions:** Either make mobile a real dialog with focus containment, or keep it clearly non-modal by removing modal-like backdrop/scroll-lock behavior.

**Comments:** Codex treated this as INFO, while Claude treated it as WARNING. Synthesis keeps it INFO because the correct fix depends on intended UX.

</details>

## Recommended Next Step
HUMAN_DECISION: The PR is already merged and this is a follow-up review pass, so a maintainer should decide whether to authorize a follow-up branch for the concrete accessibility fixes.

## Final Verdict
MINOR_ISSUES

## Action Items
- [ ] Add and update ARIA range state for the focusable drawer resize separator.
- [ ] Respect reduced-motion preference for the loading shimmer, drawer/trigger motion, and Sepo wave animation.

---
_Run: `codex` / `gpt-5.5/xhigh` / `xhigh`_

title: Add Giscus discussion drawer
